title: When Something Goes Wrong
drDigitalIntro:
Computers act up sometimes — don't panic.
• Most problems have simple fixes.
• If an app freezes (stops responding), you can force-quit it.
• Then reopen it fresh — that usually fixes the problem.
• Let's practice fixing a frozen app!

playgroundTask.goal: You fixed a frozen app by force-quitting and reopening it
playgroundTask.scenario: Notes is frozen and not responding

steps.say: Notes is frozen — it shows 'Not Responding.' Try clicking on it to see what happens.
Nothing happened — the app is stuck. Click the system menu (three dots) in the menu bar to open Force Quit.
Notes shows 'Not Responding.' Click Force Quit to close it.
Notes is closed. Click Notes in the dock to reopen it fresh.

drDigitalSuccess: You fixed the frozen app! When an app stops responding: Force Quit it, then reopen it from the dock. Most freezes are temporary.
drDigitalHint: Click the frozen window first, then use the system menu to Force Quit, and reopen from the dock.
